channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canva introduces offline mode for designing without internet
item.description: Canva, the popular online design platform, has recently rolled out a new offline mode feature, allowing users to continue working on their visual projects without an internet connection. This update i...

item.title: Highlights from the LensCulture New York 2026 Photography Exhibition
item.description: LensCulture's New York 2026 Exhibition showcased an impressive collection of photographic works from 35 award-winning photographers representing 18 countries. Held concurrently with The Photography Sh...

item.title: B.C. government postpones disclosure of World Cup hosting expenses until late May
item.description: The government of British Columbia has announced that it will delay the release of the estimated costs for hosting the upcoming World Cup until the end of May. The postponement comes amid public scrut...

item.title: Changing Alliances and Tensions in the Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Middle East is witnessing a major geopolitical reshuffle as the conflict involving the US, Iran, and Israel continues. US President Donald Trump has publicly rejected Iran's latest peace proposal,...

item.title: Two arraigned in Ado-Ekiti for assault and stealing
item.description: The Ekiti State Police Command has arraigned two suspects, Tope Omowaye and Temitope Ayokunle, at the Magistrate Court in Ado-Ekiti over allegations of assault and theft. According to ASP Yomi Osuolal...

item.title: New Beatles Museum to Open at Historic Savile Row Site Featuring Rooftop Concert Experience
item.description: Apple, the Beatles’ management company, has announced the re-acquisition of 3 Savile Row in London, the location of the band’s final rooftop performance in January 1969. This seven-storey building, wh...

item.title: Biographer Claims Trump May Fire Secretary of State Marco Rubio Due to Perceived Competence
item.description: According to journalist and Trump biographer Michael Wolff, President Donald Trump may consider firing Secretary of State Marco Rubio because Rubio has occasionally outshined the president, a trait Wo...

item.title: Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission Partners with States to Strengthen Drug Safety Monitoring
item.description: In an effort to bolster medicine safety and strengthen monitoring of adverse drug reactions (ADRs), the Indian Pharmacopoeia Commission (IPC) has signed agreements with the state pharmacy councils of ...

item.title: Tanzania Outlines Key Financial Sector Plans and Economic Updates for 2026
item.description: Tanzania’s government recently highlighted its major financial sector priorities and economic performance for 2026. Finance Minister Khamis Mussa Omar presented eight focus areas to sustain the sector...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte Remains Unconcerned Over Recent Impeachment Efforts
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte expressed indifference regarding the latest impeachment complaint filed against her. In a brief statement, she said, 'Wala akong feelings about it,' signaling that she is n...

item.title: Prateek Yadav, son of Samajwadi Party founder Mulayam Singh Yadav, dies in Lucknow at 38
item.description: Prateek Yadav, the son of Samajwadi Party founder Mulayam Singh Yadav and his second wife Sadhna Gupta, passed away at the age of 38 in Lucknow on Wednesday morning. According to reports, the Civil Ho...

item.title: Ontario monitors additional low-risk contacts amid global hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Ontario health authorities are asking seven more individuals to self-isolate in response to a global hantavirus outbreak, though these people are considered 'low-risk' contacts. This follows three hig...

item.title: Kiewit’s 85-Year Contribution to Canadian Infrastructure and Communities
item.description: For 85 years, Kiewit has played a vital role in constructing Canada’s infrastructure, from energy and transportation projects to water systems. The company began its Canadian operations in 1941 with t...

item.title: Some Canadian schools replace Mother’s and Father’s Day with International Day of Families activities
item.description: Several Canadian elementary schools, including Sage Creek School in Winnipeg and South Kelowna Elementary School in British Columbia, have chosen to shift away from traditional Mother’s and Father’s D...

item.title: Paraplegic Man Secures State-Funded Care After Lengthy Legal Struggle
item.description: After over two-and-a-half years of legal wrangling, 58-year-old Paul Gerber, a paraplegic with morbid obesity and multiple uncontrolled chronic conditions, has finally been placed at Turfhall Cheshire...

item.title: New Nelson Mandela Marathon Scheduled for October in Cape Town
item.description: With the Sanlam Cape Town Marathon shifting its annual race from October to May, organisers have identified a gap in the local running calendar, which will now be filled by the new Nelson Mandela Mara...

item.title: India hikes gold and silver import tariffs to 15% to manage trade deficit and protect rupee
item.description: On 13th May 2026, the Government of India announced an increase in import tariffs on gold and silver from 6% to 15%, in a move aimed at curbing overseas purchases of precious metals and easing pressur...

item.title: Denver Airport Incident: Man Fatally Struck by Plane During Runway Trespass
item.description: A 41-year-old man, Michael Mott, died after walking onto a Denver International Airport runway and being struck by a Frontier Airlines plane headed to Los Angeles. The collision caused the plane's eng...

item.title: South Africa’s unemployment climbs to 32.7%, affecting 8.1 million people
item.description: According to StatsSA's latest Quarterly Labour Force Survey, South Africa's unemployment rate increased to 32.7% in the first quarter of 2026, leaving 8.1 million people without jobs. The labour marke...

item.title: Siyakholwa Kuse gears up for WBC rematch against Melvin Jerusalem
item.description: Siyakholwa Kuse is set for a high-stakes rematch against WBC minimumweight champion Melvin Jerusalem at Emperors Palace this Saturday. After his points loss to Jerusalem in Manila last October, Kuse h...
